decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss, Parhelion II, and Iron-Shield Elf. It also includes ways to protect the combo with Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command, as well as ramping and fixing mana with cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Concealed Courtyard. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and synergistic interactions.

This deck focuses on using the Greasefang Parhelion combo to gain control of the game. It includes cards like Thoughtseize for disruption, Fleeting Spirit for additional damage, and Iron-Shield Elf for protection. The deck also includes support cards like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse for removal, as well as Monument to Endurance for additional defense. The goal is to quickly assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using card advantage and synergy to overwhelm opponents and come out victorious in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This Red Deck Wins aggro deck focuses on dealing quick and efficient damage to the opponent. Utilizing powerful creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, along with burn spells such as Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early on. Support from lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins adds additional pressure, while cards like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage provide added reach and removal. The deck's strategy is to be fast and aggressive, forcing the opponent to react quickly or risk falling behind.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while using burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and clear a path for damage. Stormchaser's Talent provides additional prowess triggers, while cards like Emberheart Challenger and Cori-Steel Cutter hit hard and fast. The deck also utilizes cards like Academic Dispute for card advantage and tempo plays. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain aggression to overwhelm opponents.

The main concept of this Aggro - Arclight Phoenix deck is to quickly fill up your graveyard with spells and efficiently cast Arclight Phoenix from there. With cards like Consider, Sleight of Hand, and Opt, you can dig through your deck to find the spells you need to bring back Arclight Phoenix while dealing damage to your opponent. Led by the powerful Arclight Phoenix, this deck aims to overwhelm your opponent with a combination of fast plays and hard-hitting creatures to secure victory in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays with cheap spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It utilizes a combination of burn spells, creature pump effects, and evasion through flying to apply pressure early in the game. The deck aims to overload the opponent with low-cost threats and efficient removal, while maintaining a steady flow of damage output. The synergy between the various cards allows for a fast-paced, high-energy strategy that can overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent using low-cost spells and creatures with prowess abilities. By playing efficiently and keeping up a steady stream of aggression, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly. The deck includes cards that deal direct damage to creatures and players, as well as cards that help generate card advantage. It relies on a mix of spells and creatures to keep the pressure on the opponent and secure a decisive victory.

Utilizing a high number of control spells and card draw effects, this UW Control deck aims to consistently control the game board and out value the opponent. The inclusion of Yorion as a companion allows for additional synergy with blinking effects, providing extra card advantage and flexibility in dealing with threats. The deck relies on a mix of counterspells, board wipes, and card filtering to maintain control over the game while setting up win conditions with cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and The Wandering Emperor. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes utility lands to support the control game plan.

The deck focuses on disruption and removal to control the opponent's resources and win through attrition. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also having access to creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Gifted Aetherborn for board presence. The deck also includes cards that allow for graveyard recursion and lifegain. The mana base is focused on utility lands like Castle Locthwain and Mutavault to provide additional value and flexibility.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo by using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a game-winning board state. It includes various protective measures such as Iron-Shield Elf and Seasoned Hallowblade to ensure the combo is successfully executed. Additionally, the deck includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's board and prevent interference. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo pieces and allow for consistent gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful combo while maintaining control over the game.

The deck focuses on a synergy between cards that benefit from discarding cards, such as Tinybones and Requiting Hex, while also utilizing creatures like Ashling and Carnage to control the board. Additionally, the deck includes powerful combo pieces like Glasspool Mimic and Raffine to generate additional value. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the various color requirements of the deck and ensure consistency in playing out the combo pieces. Overall, the key concept of the deck is to assemble a powerful combination of cards that work together to create a winning strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to deal direct damage to the opponent quickly and efficiently. It emphasizes aggression and tempo, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Burst Lightning to apply pressure early on. The deck also includes bounce spells like Boomerang Basics and Cori-Steel Cutter to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. With a mix of creatures with haste and burn spells, this deck aims to close out the game swiftly before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent quickly through low-cost aggressive creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, backed up by burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way or deal direct damage. The deck aims to control the board by utilizing cards like Experimental Synthesizer and Cori-Steel Cutter, while maintaining a fast tempo with cards like Stormchaser's Talent. The mana base is optimized for consistently casting spells and attacking early in the game, with options for disruption and card advantage to outpace the opponent.

This deck focuses on using the Greasefang Parhelion combo to quickly generate value and overwhelm the opponent. By consistently untapping Greasefang with effects like Fleeting Spirit and Sheltered by Ghosts, the deck can create an army of flying tokens from Parhelion II. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board while Monument to Endurance and Guardian of New Benalia provide additional support. The mana base includes various dual lands to ensure smooth and consistent gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and outpace the opponent through its combo synergy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using cheap burn spells and creatures with haste like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. Cards like Burst Lightning, Lightning Strike, and Reckless Rage help to control the board and clear the way for your creatures to attack. Monstrous Rage and Screaming Nemesis provide additional damage and pressure on the opponent, while cards like Castle Embereth and Ramunap Ruins offer additional reach and damage from lands. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent early and finish the game quickly.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with a strong board presence and limited resources. Key cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Thoughtseize help protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to deal with threats. By efficiently executing its combo strategy and controlling the game, the deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.
